blockingIssues.description: Commit a5994fc bundles three significant categories of unrelated behavioral changes beyond the stated 'behavior-preserving cleanup' scope: (1) a new OAuth runtime session layer storing access tokens in sessionStorage, (2) an allowWhileOnboarding bypass parameter on ~12 discovery setup call sites, and (3) stricter sheet-ID validation (min length 10→20). None documented in handoff. Handoff also contains a fabricated commit ID.

OAuth access token persisted in sessionStorage in plaintext via new OAUTH_RUNTIME_SESSION_STORAGE_KEY + persistRuntimeOAuthSession()/loadPersistedRuntimeOAuthSession()/clearPersistedRuntimeOAuthSession() functions. Security implications not documented.

New allowWhileOnboarding parameter changes discovery setup behavior: previously all discovery setup calls were deferred when onboarding wizard visible; now ~12 call sites bypass this check. Behavioral change not documented in handoff.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a cross-reference to .factory/library/refactoring.md so workers discover dirty-baseline commit conventions and isolated-worktree validation patterns.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: guardrail-wiring-and-refactoring-skill review found AGENTS.md has no mention of the new refactoring.md library file, which contains important conventions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document .factory/droids/ directory purpose and when workers should create or consult droids. Currently no mention of droids exists.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: maintenance-droids-and-hook-automation review found three .droid files created with no AGENTS.md reference.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reinforce 'one concern per commit' enforcement: when a worker bundles unrelated behavioral changes with cleanup, the handoff commitId should be validated as a real git object before acceptance.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: performance-and-legacy-path-cleanup bundled OAuth session management, discovery behavior changes, and validation changes with dead-code removal. Handoff contained fabricated commit ID.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the dual OAuth session storage layers: localStorage for session metadata markers and sessionStorage for runtime OAuth sessions with access tokens. Note the priority order in restoreOAuthSession().
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: performance-and-legacy-path-cleanup introduced sessionStorage layer without updating architecture.md which only mentions localStorage for OAuth session reuse.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update PIPELINE-CARDS-HANDOFF.md, docs/APP_JS_ANALYSIS.md, and TECH_DEBT_REPORT.md to reflect that TD-003 and TD-022 are now resolved (renderJobCard, currentFilter, getFilteredData removed).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: performance-and-legacy-path-cleanup review found these docs still reference removed functions as if they exist.

rejectedObservations.observation: Pre-commit hook auto-installation should be in services.yaml.
rejectedObservations.reason: services.yaml documents network services with start/stop/healthcheck. A git pre-commit hook is not a network service and doesn't fit the schema.

rejectedObservations.observation: coverage-enforcer critical test paths should be in library.
rejectedObservations.reason: The knowledge is already documented in the .droid file itself which is the appropriate location for droid-specific reference material.
Not an actionable observation - it's a positive report with no guidance update needed.

rejectedObservations.observation: Handoff commit ID fabrication in performance-and-legacy-path-cleanup.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is specific to the blocking failure already captured in blockingIssues, not reusable shared knowledge.
